steps.title: State what the claim demands
steps.content: The student claims Catlett had a talent for unifying various artistic traditions and styles. The supporting quotation must explicitly point to multiple distinct traditions or styles being brought together in one work.

steps.title: Find the quotation that names the unification
steps.content: Choice A says Catlett used "an ancient Indigenous sculpting technique" and combined "the visual aesthetic of modern Mexican muralists with that of German artist Kathe Kollwitz." That's three different traditions — Indigenous, Mexican muralism, and a German printmaker — unified in a single sculpture. Direct hit on "unifying various artistic traditions and styles."

steps.title: Eliminate the others by what they fail to show
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'ancient Indigenous... modern Mexican muralists... German artist Kathe Kollwitz': names three distinct traditions combined in one work. ✓
  • Choice B — 'overlaid fabric onto the canvas... to evoke classic images of a mother and child': describes one technique and one visual reference — that's not a fusion of multiple traditions, so it doesn't support the claim. ✗
  • Choice C — 'a stylized fist sculpted from mahogany... received renewed and well-deserved attention': praises the work's importance but names no traditions being unified. Misread to call this evidence of fusion. ✗
  • Choice D — 'linoleum cut method, depicts several notable African American women': identifies a single technique and a subject matter — nothing about combining traditions. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
